description_long: This is a vintage twin-lens reflex camera, most likely a Rolleiflex, featuring a boxy, rectangular body with a prominent viewing hood on top. The front face displays two large circular lenses stacked vertically, with the upper lens for viewing and the lower for capturing images. The camera's exterior is finished in a combination of dark brown and black, with metallic accents and dials, including a large focusing knob on one side and a winding lever on the other. The top hood opens upward, revealing a ground glass focusing screen for waist-level viewing. The back panel is marked with the Rolleiflex logo and has a film advance window and additional controls. The overall construction appears robust, with a mix of metal and leather or leatherette materials, giving it a classic, durable appearance.
description: A vintage twin-lens reflex Rolleiflex camera with a boxy body, dual front lenses, and a pop-up viewing hood. It features metallic dials, a winding lever, and a classic brown and black finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the dual stacked lenses, the Rolleiflex nameplate, and the open viewing hood.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the large focusing knob, metallic dials, and part of the hood's interior.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the Rolleiflex logo, film advance window, and the back panel details.
description_view_3: Opposite side view featuring the winding lever and additional metallic controls.
